classes.name: shinkansen.common.ShinkansenException
documentation: Base class for Shinkansen exceptions.

classes.name: shinkansen.common.FinancialInstitution
documentation:
A Financial institution with:

- fin_id_schema: The schema of the financial institution's ID
- fin_id: The ID of the financial institution

classes.name: shinkansen.common.PersonId
documentation:
A person's ID with:

- id_schema: The schema of the person's ID
- id: The ID of the person

classes.name: shinkansen.common.MessageHeader
documentation:
The header of a shinkansen message with:

- sender: The financial institution sending the message
- receiver: The financial institution receiving the message
- message_id: The ID of the message (UUID string)
- creation_date: The date the message was created (ISO8601)

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.sign
documentation:
Signs the payload with the key and returns a detached JWS.

The certificate public key must match the key used to sign.

Returns a compact detached JWS.

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.verify_detached
documentation:
Verifies a JWS signature.

Uses the x5c from the JWS to verify, and the certificate whitelist to
ensure such x5c certificate is allowed to be used by the signing party.

The whitelist should be limited to the certificates for the sender of the
message being verified.

Raises an exception if the JWS is invalid or the certificate is not
whitelisted.

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.private_key_from_pem_file
documentation:
Loads a private key from a file.

The file must be in PEM format.

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.private_key_from_pem_bytes
documentation: Loads a private key from a PEM string.

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.certificate_from_pem_file
documentation:
Loads a certificate from a file.

The file must be in PEM format.

functions.name: shinkansen.jws.certificate_from_pem_bytes
documentation: Loads a certificate from a PEM string.

classes.name: shinkansen.jws.InvalidSignature
documentation: Raised when a signature is invalid.

classes.name: shinkansen.jws.InvalidJWS
documentation: Invalid JWS Object (e.g: missing headers) when verifying

classes.name: shinkansen.jws.CertificateNotWhitelisted
documentation: Certificate not found in the whitelist when verifying

classes.name: shinkansen.jws.KeyMismatch
documentation: Certificate and key don't match when signing.

variables.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutResponse
documentation: for backwards compatibility

variables.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutMessageHeader
documentation: for backwards compatibility

classes.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutDebtor
documentation:
The debtor (origin) of a payout with:

- name: The name of the debtor
- identification: The ID of the debtor
- financial_institution: The financial institution of the debtor
- account: The account of the debtor
- account_type: The type of account of the debtor
- email: The email of the debtor

classes.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutCreditor
documentation:
The creditor (destination) of a payout with:

- name: The name of the creditor
- identification: The ID of the creditor
- financial_institution: The financial institution of the creditor
- account: The account of the creditor
- account_type: The type of account of the creditor
- email: The email of the creditor

classes.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutTransaction
documentation:
A payout transaction with:

- transaction_id: The ID of the transaction (UUID string)
- currency: The currency of the transaction
- amount: The amount of the transaction (string)
- description: The description of the transaction
- execution_date: The date the transaction should preferably be executed
- debtor: The debtor of the transaction
- creditor: The creditor of the transaction

classes.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutHttpResponseError
documentation:
An error in the payout sync response with:

- error_code: The error code
- error_message: Human-readable error message

classes.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutHttpResponse

methods.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutHttpResponse.from_http_response
documentation: Return a PayoutSyncResponse from a HTTP response

The http response received right after posting a payout message, with:
- http_status_code: The HTTP code of the response
- transaction ids: A dict mapping transaction ids to Shinkansen's transaction id
- errors: A list of `PayoutHttpResponseError`s

documentation: Returns the message as a JSON object

documentation: Return a message from a JSON string

methods.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutMessage.id
documentation: Returns the ID of the message

methods.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutMessage.sign_and_send
documentation: Signs the message and sends it to the Shinkansen API

methods.name: shinkansen.payouts.PayoutMessage.send
documentation: Sends the message to the Shinkansen API

A payout message with:

- header: The header of the message

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.UnexpectedSender
documentation: The sender of the message is different than expected.

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.UnexpectedReceiver
documentation: The receiver of the message is different than expected.

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.Response

methods.name: shinkansen.responses.Response.for_transaction_type
documentation: Decorator to register a response class for a transaction type

Generic response with:

- response_id: The id of the response itself.
- transaction_type: The type of the original transaction this response corresponds to.
- transaction_id: The id of the original transaction for this response corresponds to.
- shinkansen_transaction_id: The shinkansen assigned id for the original transaction.
- shinkansen_transaction_status: The resulting status of the transaction
- shinkansen_transaction_message: Human readable message further explaining the statuse of the transaction
- response_status: The status of the response. Valid values depend on the transaction type
- response_message: Human readable message explaining the response status

Concrete subclasses can add other fields

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.PayoutResponse
documentation:
Payout response with:

- response_id: The id of the response itself.
- transaction_id: The id of the original transaction for this response corresponds to.
- shinkansen_transaction_id: The shinkansen assigned id for the original transaction.
- shinkansen_transaction_status: The resulting status of the transaction
- shinkansen_transaction_message: Human readable message further explaining the statuse of the transaction
- response_status: The status of the response. Valid values depend on the transaction type
- response_message: Human readable message explaining the response status

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.PayinResponse
documentation:
Payin response with:

- response_id: The id of the response itself.
- transaction_id: The id of the original transaction for this response corresponds to.
- shinkansen_transaction_id: The shinkansen assigned id for the original transaction.
- shinkansen_transaction_status: The resulting status of the transaction
- shinkansen_transaction_message: Human readable message further explaining the statuse of the transaction
- response_status: The status of the response. Valid values depend on the transaction type
- response_message: Human readable message explaining the response status
- expected_settlement_date: When funds are expected to arrive
- additional_info: A dict with additional info about the transaction

classes.name: shinkansen.responses.ResponseMessage

methods.name: shinkansen.responses.ResponseMessage.as_json
documentation: Returns the message as a JSON object

methods.name: shinkansen.responses.ResponseMessage.from_json
documentation: Returns a message from a JSON string

methods.name: shinkansen.responses.ResponseMessage.id
documentation: Returns the id of the message

methods.name: shinkansen.responses.ResponseMessage.verify
documentation:
Verifies the message signature and checks that the sender and receiver
are as expected.

Raises an exception if the verification fails:

- shinkansen.jws.InvalidJWS -> Invalid JWS object
- shinkansen.jws.InvalidSignature -> Invalid signature
- shinkansen.jws.CertificateNotWhitelisted -> Unexpected sender certificate
- shinkansen.responses.UnexpectedSender -> Unexpected sender
- shinkansen.responses.UnexpectedReceiver -> Unexpected receiver

The sender is almost always SHINKANSEN and the receiver is your company
('financial institution'). It's important to check those fields to avoid
replay attacks.

A response message with:

- header: The header of the message
- responses: A list of responses contained in the message

classes.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinDebtor
documentation:
The debtor (origin) of a payin with the following fields:

- name: The name of the debtor (optional)
- identification: The ID of the debtor (optional)
- financial_institution: The financial institution of the debtor (optional)
- account: The account of the debtor (optional)
- account_type: The type of account of the debtor (optional)
- email: The email of the debtor (optional)

classes.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinCreditor
documentation:
The creditor (destination) of a payin with:

- name: The name of the creditor
- identification: The ID of the creditor
- financial_institution: The financial institution of the creditor
- account: The account of the creditor
- account_type: The type of account of the creditor
- email: The email of the creditor

classes.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinTransaction
documentation:
A payin transaction with:

- payin_type: Either "interactive_payment" or "automated_payment" or "expected_payment"
- interactive_payment_provider: The provider of the interactive payment (optional)
- interactive_payment_success_redirect_url: The URL to redirect to after a successful interactive payment (only required for interactive payments)
- interactive_payment_failure_redirect_url: The URL to redirect to after a failed interactive payment (only required for interactive payments)
- transaction_id: The ID of the transaction (UUID string)
- currency: The currency of the transaction
- amount: The amount of the transaction (string) (optional)
- description: The description of the transaction
- expiration_date: The last date the transaction can be executed, after which the payment will be considered as failed (optional)
- debtor: The debtor of the transaction
- creditor: The creditor of the transaction

classes.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinHttpResponseError
documentation:
An error in the payin sync response with:

- error_code: The error code
- error_message: Human-readable error message

classes.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinHttpResponse

methods.name: shinkansen.payins.PayinHttpResponse.from_http_response
documentation: Return a PayinHttpResponse from a HTTP response

The http response received right after posting a payin message, with:
- http_status_code: The HTTP code of the response
- transaction ids: A dict mapping transaction ids to Shinkansen's transaction id
- interactive_payment_urls: A dict mapping transaction ids to interactive payment URLs
- errors: A list of `PayinHttpResponseError`s
